weather_report:
Subiaco settles into a clear, unhurried night, the sky mostly open with only a few clouds idling by. Streetlights are crisp, stars peek through, and the air feels clean and uncomplicated. It’s not a night for sun, of course; the UV index is effectively asleep, and the world is trimmed in dark blue edges. If you step out, a light layer will do, the kind you forget you’re wearing until a soft breeze reminds you.

Through the late hours and into morning, this tidy sky holds: expect a bright, mostly sunny day tempered by scattered, harmless cloud. Visibility will be sharp, colors a touch more vivid. UV will stay negligible at dawn and then build with the light, so keep sunglasses and a dab of sunscreen at hand if you linger outdoors. Dress in easy layers—something breathable for the day, a cardigan or light jacket for the edges. It’s a good time for small, ordinary errands: a quiet loop around the block, airing out the rooms, watering the basil, a gentle cycle to the shops, or hanging a short line of washing to meet the first clean sun.
